alerts.title: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz, threatens missile strikes on Israel over Lebanon ceasefire violations
alerts.summary: Multiple outlets (NBC News, NPR, PBS, Ynetnews) report Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz in response to ongoing Israeli strikes killing 32+ in Lebanon despite ceasefire agreement. Iran threatens imminent missile fire on Israel and halts US-Iran nuclear talks scheduled for Sunday in Switzerland. Escalation directly threatens global oil markets and diplomatic resolution.

alerts.title: Iran uranium enrichment at 60% U-235; breakout window 1-2 weeks amid failed diplomacy
alerts.summary: Global metrics confirm Iran has enriched uranium to 60% U-235 with nuclear breakout timeframe narrowed to approximately 1-2 weeks. UN atomic energy agency reports technical work underway on dilution and stockpile disposal. Collapsed US-Iran talks due to Lebanon fighting create imminent risk to deal implementation and verification protocols.

alerts.title: Israeli strikes kill 32+ in Lebanon; ceasefire collapses amid escalating Hezbollah-IDF combat
alerts.summary: Al Jazeera, Reuters, and BBC report Israeli airstrikes killing at least 32 civilians in southern Lebanon (33.86°N, 35.91°E) and Beirut suburbs (34.1°N, 35.23°E) hours after ceasefire announcement. MSF describes conditions in Nabatieh as 'death trap.' Lebanon confirms 47+ total killed; concurrent Hezbollah-IDF exchanges intensify despite diplomatic framework.

alerts.title: US deploys three carriers and amphibious forces to Red Sea and Arabian Sea amid Iran escalation
alerts.summary: Assets feed confirms USS Dwight D. Eisenhower (Red Sea, 15.69°N, 42.49°E), USS Abraham Lincoln (Arabian Sea, 20.89°N, 59.71°E), USS Bataan amphibious, and multiple destroyers (Carney, Mason, Laboon) positioned in Red Sea. USS Gerald R. Ford in Mediterranean (34.21°N, 30.28°E). Deployment surge coincides with Iran's Strait closure threat and nuclear breakout window.
alerts.region: Red Sea / Arabian Sea / Persian Gulf

alerts.title: US negative sentiment surges to 51% amid Iran conflict escalation and Trump administration messaging
alerts.summary: Sentiment analysis shows US negative sentiment at 51% (vs. 41% positive), with 'Trump' (91 mentions), 'Iran' (32), 'deal' (20), and 'war' (9) dominating word cloud. Trend reflects public anxiety over failed nuclear diplomacy, Lebanon casualty reports, and Strait closure threat. Shift indicates potential domestic political pressure on administration's Middle East strategy.

analysis_note: Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz and threat of imminent missile strikes, combined with collapse of US-Iran nuclear talks and uranium enrichment at 60% U-235 (1-2 week breakout window), signal critical escalation moment; Israeli ceasefire violations in Lebanon have triggered cascading diplomatic and military breakdown with direct threats to regional stability and global energy security.
